invalid_attempts.reason: CLI returned tool-call style text without closeout verdict JSON; not counted as a valid pack-level review.
Review input omitted untracked implementation files and returned blocking coverage text instead of required JSON; not counted as a valid pack-level review.

review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --disallowedTools "Read,Bash,Edit,Write,MultiEdit,Glob,Grep,LS" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-096-claude-review-output-valid-3.json

findings.title: Executor canonicalizes all blocks through 'routed' state
findings.description: executeCoreDomainWorkflow always builds the blocked state_path as requested->normalized->prechecked->routed->blocked regardless of the actual blocking stage (e.g. lock_unavailable conceptually blocks at precheck). The contract declares normalized->blocked and prechecked->blocked transitions that the executor never emits; only requested->blocked (catch path) and routed->blocked are exercised. All emitted transitions are individually valid, so this is a modeling-fidelity gap, not a functional defect.
findings.recommendation: Either emit the stage-accurate blocked transition or document that the executor intentionally canonicalizes to the routed->blocked path so the unused contract transitions are not read as dead spec.

findings.title: Fixture source_unit_range narrower than pack range
findings.description: CORE_DOMAIN_SYNTHETIC_FIXTURE_PACK_BINDING.source_unit_range is 'RP01.P02.M06.S01-RP01.P02.M06.S06', a subset of the pack/workflow range 'RP01.P02.M04.S07-RP01.P02.M06.S06'. Likely intentional (fixtures live in the M06 sub-band) but the divergence from CORE_DOMAIN_WORKFLOW_PACK_BINDING.range could confuse range-coverage tracking.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the sub-range is intentional and add a brief comment, or align the fixture binding range with the pack range.

findings.title: Forbidden persistence target collapses to generic validation error
findings.description: A disallowed persistence_target ('database') causes createCoreDomainPersistenceBoundary to throw, which is caught and mapped to the generic 'core_domain_workflow_validation_error' claim. The boundary correctly fails closed (writes_product_state stays false, status blocked), but the specific product/DB-write boundary violation is not distinguishable in blocked_claims.
findings.recommendation: Optionally emit a dedicated blocked_claim (e.g. 'persistence_boundary_violation') so boundary breaches are observable separately from generic validation errors. Non-blocking since it already fails closed.

findings.title: Permission-reference denial treated as retryable
findings.description: createCoreDomainRetryPolicy marks 'permission_reference_denies_operation' as retryable alongside 'lock_unavailable'. A referenced permission denial is not normally resolved by a synthetic retry without a permission change, so scheduling retry_scheduled for deny is semantically questionable. Synthetic-only, so no boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Reconsider whether a referenced deny should be retryable, or document the synthetic-retry rationale.

notes: All hard boundaries upheld: workflow sets evaluates_runtime_permission/writes_audit_event/writes_product_state=false throughout, persistence restricted to none|synthetic_fixture_store and fails closed on disallowed targets, fixtures are synthetic with uses_real_client_data=false; HRX/LDIP untouched.
Permission handling is reference-binding only (intake.permission_precheck.evaluated=false; deny mapped to blocked_claim), consistent with the stated scope.

contracts JSON state_transitions and blocked_claims match workflow.js CORE_DOMAIN_WORKFLOW_TRANSITIONS and emitted claims by inspection; validate script and tests reference exports now surfaced via index.js.
All emitted state_path sequences (happy, blocked, catch) consist of individually valid transitions; assertCoreDomainWorkflowTransition guards the happy/blocked paths.
No shell/eval/external IO or injection surface; snapshot_ref is a plain synthetic string.
